description:
You are acting as an on-call support engineer who has just logged in to an application server.  
    Your goal is to collect a very small diagnostics bundle consisting of one summary report and one
    config change.  Everything you need already exists under /home/user.

    1. Log analysis  
       • The original log file is /home/user/logs/system.log.  
       • Scan that file and derive the following three facts:
         a) ERROR_COUNT – the total number of lines whose third column is literally the word “ERROR”.  
         b) FIRST_ERROR_TIMESTAMP – the full date-and-time string (columns 1 and 2) that appears on
            the very first “ERROR” line in the file (i.e. the earliest error in chronological order).  
         c) ERROR_CODES – a comma-separated list (no spaces) of the unique error codes that appear in square
            brackets on ERROR lines, sorted in ascending (lexicographic) order.

    2. Report file  
       • Create the directory /home/user/diagnostics if it does not yet exist.  
       • Write a file /home/user/diagnostics/summary.log that contains exactly three lines in the
         following fixed format (no extra spaces, no headers):
            ERROR_COUNT=<integer>
            FIRST_ERROR_TIMESTAMP=<YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS>
            ERROR_CODES=<code1>,<code2>,<code3>[,...]

    3. Config update  
       • The application configuration file is /home/user/config/app.conf.  
       • Inside that file there is a line that is currently commented out:
            #diagnostics=false
       • Replace that line so that it becomes the active setting
            diagnostics=true
         (i.e. remove the leading “#”, change the value to “true”, leave other lines untouched).

    Tools you must use  
       * Use awk for all log parsing tasks (steps 1a–1c).  
       * Use sed for the in-place edit of app.conf.  
       * Besides directory creation and redirection, no other utilities are required; everything can
         be achieved in three to four shell commands total.

    Verification notes  
       • The automated grader will check the exact byte-for-byte contents of
         /home/user/diagnostics/summary.log as well as the final state of /home/user/config/app.conf.  
       • The log analysis must be performed dynamically by your commands; do NOT hard-code values in
         the summary file.

    When you are done, /home/user/diagnostics/summary.log must exist and /home/user/config/app.conf
    must have the updated diagnostics=true line as specified.
truth:
Initial files present before the agent starts:

    1. /home/user/logs/system.log
       2023-05-01 10:15:23 INFO  Startup complete
       2023-05-01 10:15:24 ERROR [E101] Failed to load module alpha
       2023-05-01 10:15:25 WARN  Low memory
       2023-05-01 10:15:26 ERROR [E102] Connection timeout
       2023-05-01 10:15:27 INFO  Health check passed
       2023-05-01 10:15:28 ERROR [E101] Failed to load module alpha
       2023-05-01 10:15:29 ERROR [E103] Disk full

    2. /home/user/config/app.conf
       #diagnostics=false
       loglevel=INFO
       path=/var/app/

    Expected final state after the agent finishes:

    1. /home/user/diagnostics/summary.log (permissions 0644, any owner)
       ERROR_COUNT=4
       FIRST_ERROR_TIMESTAMP=2023-05-01 10:15:24
       ERROR_CODES=E101,E102,E103

    2. /home/user/config/app.conf
       diagnostics=true
       loglevel=INFO
       path=/var/app/
